page: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose cloud server powered by a KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Core Processor based on the Haswell architecture with 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 120.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 3.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 400 GB SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics are relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core execution is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the middle 50%. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly, ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations also align with average tier expectations.

Given the dedicated 32-core architecture and balanced memory-to-CPU ratio, this server is suited for multi-threaded workloads that can leverage parallel processing, such as static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose database operations. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM text generation, where it exhibits poor to weak performance. The inclusion of local SSD storage and a 10 Gbps network baseline provides a stable foundation for standard enterprise applications without requiring specialized GPU acceleration.
description: The OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated x86_64 Intel physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 120.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 400 GB local SSD. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct profile: while single-core metrics are weak, multi-core capabilities are solid, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching the top 25% and multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores landing in the middle 50%. This resource distribution makes the b2-120 highly suitable for parallelized workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, though it is less efficient for single-threaded tasks or large-scale language model inference.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 400 GB local SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance and 10 Gbps network bandwidth.

tagline: A dedicated thirty-two core server with balanced memory and local SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and general-purpose applications.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel physical cores at 2.0 GHz
120 GB RAM and 400 GB bundled local SSD
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with 1 public IPv4 address
Strong multi-threaded performance, including top-tier bzip3 decompression
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and databases
